assignment: Resolve the exact session deactivation cleanup contract using read-only probes. Keep current-owned activation and the verified 6-of-6 behavior. Determine how context cleanup cancels and joins live work without beginning or committing durable finish.
required_checks: Probe close with no active work, one active work item, multiple active siblings, an already settled item, explicit checkpoint before close, and explicit finish before close.
For ordinary close, assert no store commit and unchanged durable session status and version.
Distinguish disposable in-memory attempt settlement from durable session business-state mutation and state the boundary precisely.
Probe idempotent repeated deactivation and concurrent explicit finish versus deactivation ordering.
Decide whether the smallest SDK contract is a Runtime deactivation method, a resource cleanup closure, or another existing method composition. Do not add a Lite primitive.
State exact cancellation, join, error, and cleanup-order semantics that progression tests must enforce.
Produce a complete executable DKR checkpoint identity and wall structure, but do not self-accept it.
forbidden: Product code, tests, docs, manifests, git history, PR state, external services, frame, tree, or another worker's artifacts
A Lite primitive, nearest-context ownership, start, spawn, shared scope factory, or hidden cleanup effect
Weakening cleanup_business_state_mutation_count or treating the current breach as acceptable
required_outputs: workers/dkr-1-cleanup/progress.jsonl via the absolute OKRA store helper worker-report command
terminal_condition: Finish when replayable cases choose one exact deactivation contract, preserve the verified activation model, and leave downstream blocked for independent validation.
